name: VS Code
analogy: Like a carpenter's workbench — where you write, edit, and organize all your code
whatItDoes: VS Code (Visual Studio Code) is a free code editor made by Microsoft. It provides syntax highlighting, intelligent autocomplete, a built-in terminal, Git integration, and a massive library of extensions — all in one lightweight application. It works with virtually every programming language and is the most widely used code editor in the world.
whyYouUseIt: VS Code is where all the SafetyIQ and HSE app code gets written. The TypeScript integration gives me real-time type errors as I type, and the Tailwind CSS IntelliSense extension shows me autocomplete suggestions for class names. The integrated terminal means I can run 'npm run dev' without switching to a separate window.
whenToSwap: Cursor is a VS Code fork with AI assistance built directly into the editor — it is worth trying if you want AI suggestions that understand your whole codebase. WebStorm is a paid editor from JetBrains with more powerful refactoring tools, better suited to large team projects. Vim/Neovim is for developers who want maximum speed and live in the terminal.
